content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Return explicit inserted, unchanged, revised, conflicting, quarantined, or rejected results from atomic constraints and comparison.

hints: For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Upsert Outcome Union model · Theory Feed Ts Upse…, name the reader, publisher, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, tenant, URL, unknown XML, runtime validation, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, PostgreSQL, Express, handle, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Ts Upsert Outcome Union precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Upsert Outcome Union model · Theory Feed Ts Upse…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 7739 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove scoped identity, typed upsert outcomes, revisions, tombstones, and replay convergence; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Return explicit inserted, unchanged, revised, conflicting, quarantined, or rejected results from atomic constraints and comparison.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Upsert Outcome Union model · Theory Feed Ts Upse…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE is self-explanatory idempotency.
